title: pleopeltis polylepis
intro: Pleopeltis polylepis, also known as the resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is known for its unique ability to dry out and revive itself when exposed to water, earning it the nickname "resurrection fern". The medicinal properties of Pleopeltis polylepis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis polylepis can be found in teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are believed to provide health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing.
